title: juniperus coahuilensis subsp. arizonica
intro: Juniperus coahuilensis subsp. arizonica, also known as Arizona Juniper, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can be used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Juniperus coahuilensis subsp. arizonica include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Juniper berries can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their effects.
